title: Add test-service-images CI job with stestr support
description:
**Size:** 📦 medium
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** high
**Source:** Proposed for 'When the service images are built (build-imag'

Add a `test-service-images` matrix job to `.github/workflows/build-images.yaml` that runs upstream OpenStack unit tests before images are signed/published. The job reuses the existing source-checkout, patch-application, and constraint-override steps from `build-service-images`. It installs the service with test extras (`<service>[test] stestr testtools`) via `uv pip install` in the venv-builder container, then runs `stestr run` with an optional `--exclude-list` flag pointing to `releases/<release>/test-excludes/<service>.txt`. Matrix starts with `{service: [keystone], release: ["2025.2"]}`. Job dependencies: `needs: [build-base-images, verify-base-images]`. Update `verify-service-images` to also require `test-service-images`. Subunit test output uploaded as CI artifacts. Create initial `releases/2025.2/test-excludes/keystone.txt` exclusion file. Update `verify_release_config.sh` to accept the optional `test-excludes/` subdirectory and validate file format. Update `verify_build_images_workflow.sh` to validate the new job structure and dependency wiring.

**Rationale:** Currently there is no validation that upstream OpenStack code combined with patches and constraint overrides produces functional code. A broken patch or incompatible constraint override can ship broken service images. Running upstream unit tests catches regressions before images reach signing and publication, reducing the blast radius of upstream source integration failures.

**Affected Areas:**
- .github/workflows/build-images.yaml
- releases/2025.2/test-excludes/keystone.txt
- tests/container-images/verify_release_config.sh
- tests/container-images/verify_build_images_workflow.sh

stories.title: CI pipeline catches broken patches before image publication
stories.want: upstream OpenStack unit tests to run against the patched and constraint-overridden source before images are signed and published
stories.so_that: broken patches or incompatible constraint overrides are caught before they ship as container images
criteria: test-service-images job exists in build-images.yaml with matrix {service: [keystone], release: ["2025.2"]}
Job depends on build-base-images and verify-base-images (same as build-service-images)
Job reuses source-checkout, patch-application, and constraint-override step patterns from build-service-images
stestr run executes inside the venv-builder container with service[test] and test dependencies installed
Job runs on both PR and push events (no PR-skip guard)

stories.title: CI pipeline supports per-service test exclusions
stories.want: to exclude known-flaky or environment-dependent upstream tests via per-service exclusion files
stories.so_that: the test job produces reliable results without false negatives from tests that cannot pass in CI
criteria: Exclusion file at releases/<release>/test-excludes/<service>.txt is optional
When present, stestr run uses --exclude-list flag pointing to the exclusion file
When absent, stestr run executes without --exclude-list
File uses stestr regex format: one pattern per line, # for comments, blank lines allowed
Initial keystone.txt exclusion file exists at releases/2025.2/test-excludes/keystone.txt

stories.title: CI pipeline provides test artifacts for debugging
stories.want: subunit test output uploaded as CI artifacts
stories.so_that: failed test runs can be investigated post-hoc without re-running the pipeline
criteria: stestr subunit output is captured to a file during test execution
Subunit output is uploaded as a GitHub Actions artifact via actions/upload-artifact
Artifact is available even when the test step fails (if: always() or equivalent)
Artifact name includes service and release for disambiguation in matrix runs

stories.title: Service image verification gates on test results
stories.want: verify-service-images to require test-service-images to pass
stories.so_that: untested service images are never verified and published
criteria: verify-service-images needs array includes test-service-images
verify-service-images needs array still includes build-service-images
verify-service-images continues to run only on push events (existing if: guard unchanged)

stories.title: Verification scripts validate new job and config structure
stories.want: verify_release_config.sh and verify_build_images_workflow.sh to validate the new test-excludes directory and test-service-images job
stories.so_that: structural regressions in the workflow or release config are caught by the verify-container-images pipeline
criteria: verify_release_config.sh accepts optional test-excludes/ subdirectory without failure
verify_release_config.sh validates test-excludes file format when files exist
verify_build_images_workflow.sh validates test-service-images job structure (matrix, needs, permissions, timeout, runner)
verify_build_images_workflow.sh validates test-service-images step-level structure (source ref, patches, constraints, stestr, artifacts)
verify_build_images_workflow.sh validates updated verify-service-images dependency wiring
Existing multi-job tests (timeout, runner, job count) updated to include test-service-images

requirements.description: The workflow SHALL define a test-service-images job with a matrix matching build-service-images (service: [keystone], release: ["2025.2"])
requirements.rationale: The test job must cover the same service x release combinations as the build job to validate every image variant

scenarios.name: Job exists with correct name
scenarios.then: a job named test-service-images exists under .jobs
and_then: the job has a strategy.matrix block

scenarios.name: Matrix includes keystone and 2025.2
scenarios.then: matrix.service contains keystone and matrix.release contains 2025.2
and_then: matrix values match build-service-images exactly

scenarios.name: Matrix uses fail-fast: false
scenarios.when: additional services are added to the matrix in the future
scenarios.then: a failure in one service does not cancel tests for other services
and_then: fail-fast is explicitly set to false

requirements.description: The test-service-images job SHALL depend on build-base-images and verify-base-images
requirements.rationale: The job uses the venv-builder image from build-base-images, which must be verified before use

scenarios.name: Correct dependency chain
scenarios.when: the test-service-images job needs array is inspected
scenarios.then: it contains both build-base-images and verify-base-images
and_then: the job does not depend on build-service-images

scenarios.name: Job waits for base images
scenarios.then: test-service-images is skipped
and_then: verify-service-images is also skipped due to transitive dependency

scenarios.name: Job consumes venv-builder output
scenarios.when: the job steps reference the venv-builder image
scenarios.then: they use needs.build-base-images.outputs.venv-builder-image
and_then: the image reference is digest-pinned

requirements.description: The test-service-images job SHALL reuse source-checkout, patch-application, and constraint-override step patterns from build-service-images
requirements.rationale: Tests must run against the same source+patches+constraints combination that the image build uses, ensuring test fidelity

scenarios.name: Source ref resolved from source-refs.yaml
scenarios.then: it reads the version from releases/<release>/source-refs.yaml via yq
and_then: null/empty values cause the step to exit 1 with an error annotation

scenarios.name: Upstream source checked out at resolved ref
scenarios.then: openstack/<service> is cloned at the resolved ref into src/<service>
and_then: fetch-depth: 1 is used for shallow clone

scenarios.name: Patches applied conditionally
scenarios.then: git apply runs against the upstream checkout
and_then: the step is skipped when no patch files exist (hashFiles guard)

scenarios.name: Constraint overrides applied
scenarios.then: scripts/apply-constraint-overrides.sh is called with the release
and_then: the step uses env vars for matrix values (expression injection defense)

requirements.description: The test-service-images job SHALL install the service with test extras via uv pip install in the venv-builder container
requirements.rationale: Test dependencies must be installed in the same venv environment used for builds to match the production dependency resolution

scenarios.name: Test dependencies installed
scenarios.when: the install step runs inside the venv-builder container
scenarios.then: uv pip install installs <service>[test] stestr testtools with --constraint upper-constraints.txt
and_then: the --prefix /var/lib/openstack flag targets the shared virtualenv

scenarios.name: Constraint file applied during install
scenarios.then: the releases/<release>/upper-constraints.txt file is used as --constraint
and_then: constraint overrides have already been applied by a prior step

scenarios.name: Source directory accessible in container
scenarios.when: the docker run mounts are configured
scenarios.then: the service source directory is mounted into the container at a writable path
and_then: stestr can create .stestr/ in the working directory

requirements.description: The test-service-images job SHALL execute stestr run with an optional --exclude-list flag pointing to releases/<release>/test-excludes/<service>.txt
requirements.rationale: stestr is the standard OpenStack test runner; --exclude-list allows skipping known-flaky tests

scenarios.name: stestr runs with exclude list when file exists
scenarios.then: stestr run is invoked with --exclude-list pointing to the file
and_then: tests matching patterns in the file are skipped

scenarios.name: stestr runs without exclude list when file absent
scenarios.then: stestr run is invoked without --exclude-list
and_then: all discovered tests execute

scenarios.name: stestr init runs before stestr run
scenarios.then: stestr init is called to set up the test repository before stestr run
and_then: the .stestr/ directory is created in the working directory

requirements.description: The test-service-images job SHALL upload subunit test output as CI artifacts
requirements.rationale: Subunit output enables post-hoc debugging of test failures without re-running the pipeline

scenarios.name: Subunit output captured
scenarios.then: stestr last --subunit output is written to a file on the runner
and_then: the file is accessible after the docker run step completes

scenarios.name: Artifact uploaded
scenarios.then: the subunit file is uploaded via actions/upload-artifact
and_then: artifact name includes service and release for matrix disambiguation

scenarios.name: Artifact available on failure
scenarios.then: the artifact upload step still runs (if: always() guard)
and_then: the subunit output is available for debugging

requirements.description: The verify-service-images job SHALL depend on both build-service-images and test-service-images
requirements.rationale: Service images should only be verified (and thus eligible for publication) after both build and test jobs pass

scenarios.name: Updated needs array
scenarios.then: it contains both build-service-images and test-service-images
and_then: the existing if: guard for push-only remains unchanged

scenarios.name: Test failure blocks verification
scenarios.when: test-service-images fails for a matrix entry
scenarios.then: verify-service-images is skipped for that entry
and_then: the service image is not verified or published

scenarios.name: Both jobs must pass
scenarios.then: verify-service-images does not run
and_then: the built image remains unverified

requirements.description: Test exclusion files SHALL use stestr-compatible regex format with one pattern per line and # comment support
requirements.rationale: stestr --exclude-list expects a specific file format; invalid format causes silent test skips or stestr errors

scenarios.name: Valid exclusion file
scenarios.when: a test-excludes/<service>.txt file contains one regex per line with # comments
scenarios.then: stestr correctly excludes matching tests
and_then: blank lines and comment lines are ignored

scenarios.name: Empty exclusion file
scenarios.when: a test-excludes/<service>.txt file exists but contains only comments or blank lines
scenarios.then: no tests are excluded
and_then: stestr runs all discovered tests

scenarios.name: Initial keystone exclusion file
scenarios.then: it exists with a header comment explaining the format
and_then: the file is valid stestr exclude-list format

requirements.description: verify_release_config.sh SHALL accept the optional test-excludes/ subdirectory and validate file format when present
requirements.rationale: Release config validation must cover the new test-excludes files to catch format errors before CI runs

scenarios.name: Directory absent is acceptable
scenarios.then: verify_release_config.sh passes without error
and_then: a PASS message indicates the directory is optional

scenarios.name: Valid directory with .txt files
scenarios.when: releases/<release>/test-excludes/ contains only .txt files named after services in source-refs.yaml
scenarios.then: verify_release_config.sh passes all format checks
and_then: PASS messages confirm file naming and format validation

scenarios.name: File with invalid name rejected
scenarios.when: test-excludes/ contains a file whose name (sans .txt) is not a service in source-refs.yaml
scenarios.then: verify_release_config.sh reports a FAIL
and_then: the error message identifies the invalid filename

requirements.description: verify_build_images_workflow.sh SHALL validate the test-service-images job structure, steps, and dependency wiring
requirements.rationale: Structural regression tests prevent accidental removal or misconfiguration of the test job

scenarios.name: Job structure validated
scenarios.then: it validates test-service-images has correct matrix, needs, permissions, timeout, runner, and fail-fast
and_then: PASS/FAIL messages are printed for each check

scenarios.name: Step-level validation
scenarios.then: it validates source-ref, patches, constraints, stestr, and artifact upload steps exist
and_then: step properties (env vars, conditional guards) are checked

scenarios.name: Dependency wiring validated
scenarios.then: it validates verify-service-images needs includes test-service-images
and_then: existing multi-job tests (timeout, runner, job count) include test-service-images

requirements.description: The test-service-images job SHALL use least-privilege permissions: contents: read and packages: read
requirements.rationale: The test job only needs to checkout code and pull images from GHCR; no write access is required

scenarios.name: Read-only permissions
scenarios.when: the test-service-images permissions block is inspected
scenarios.then: contents is read and packages is read
and_then: no id-token, attestations, or security-events permissions are granted

scenarios.name: No registry write
scenarios.then: it does not push any images or artifacts to GHCR
and_then: packages: read is sufficient for docker pull

requirements.description: The test-service-images job SHALL follow existing CI conventions: timeout-minutes, runs-on: ubuntu-latest, env vars for matrix values
requirements.rationale: Consistent conventions ensure maintainability and prevent expression injection vulnerabilities

scenarios.name: Timeout set
scenarios.then: it has a numeric timeout value
and_then: the timeout is reasonable for running upstream unit tests (e.g. 30-60 minutes)

scenarios.name: Runner is ubuntu-latest
scenarios.when: the test-service-images runs-on value is inspected
scenarios.then: it is ubuntu-latest
and_then: matching all other jobs in the workflow

scenarios.name: Expression injection defense
scenarios.when: run: blocks in test-service-images are inspected
scenarios.then: matrix values are passed via env: blocks, not direct ${{ }} interpolation in run:
and_then: matching the pattern established in build-service-images

tasks.title: Create releases/2025.2/test-excludes/keystone.txt exclusion file (CC-0034 REQ-008)
tasks.description: Create the directory releases/2025.2/test-excludes/ and the initial keystone.txt file. The file should have a SPDX header comment, a comment block explaining the stestr --exclude-list format (one regex per line, # for comments, blank lines ignored), and be otherwise empty (no excluded tests initially). Follow the SPDX header pattern used in other repository files.

tasks.title: Add test-service-images job to build-images.yaml with source preparation, test execution, and artifact upload (CC-0034 REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-005, REQ-006, REQ-011, REQ-012)
tasks.description: Add the full test-service-images job to .github/workflows/build-images.yaml between build-service-images and verify-service-images. The job has: matrix {service: [keystone], release: ["2025.2"]}, fail-fast: false, needs: [build-base-images, verify-base-images], permissions: {contents: read, packages: read}, timeout-minutes: 60, runs-on: ubuntu-latest. Steps: (1) checkout this repo, (2) resolve source ref from source-refs.yaml via yq with null/empty guard, (3) checkout openstack/<service> at resolved ref into src/<service> with fetch-depth: 1, (4) apply patches conditionally with hashFiles guard, (5) apply constraint overrides via scripts/apply-constraint-overrides.sh, (6) docker login to GHCR, (7) run tests in venv-builder container — docker run with volume mounts for source, upper-constraints.txt, optional test-excludes file, and results directory; inside the container: uv pip install --prefix /var/lib/openstack --constraint upper-constraints.txt './<service>[test]' stestr testtools, then stestr init, then stestr run with conditional --exclude-list, then stestr last --subunit to capture output, (8) upload subunit artifact via actions/upload-artifact with if: always() guard and name including matrix.service and matrix.release. All run: blocks use env: for matrix values (expression injection defense). Steps 1-5 follow existing patterns from build-service-images lines 294-328.

tasks.title: Update verify-service-images needs to include test-service-images (CC-0034 REQ-007)
tasks.description: In .github/workflows/build-images.yaml, change verify-service-images needs from [build-service-images] to [build-service-images, test-service-images]. Do not change the existing if: guard or any other job properties.

tasks.title: Add test-excludes validation to verify_release_config.sh (CC-0034 REQ-009)
tasks.description: Add a new test function test_test_excludes_directory_valid() to tests/container-images/verify_release_config.sh. The function: (1) checks if releases/2025.2/test-excludes/ exists — if not, PASS with 'optional directory absent'; (2) if present, validates all files have .txt extension; (3) validates each filename (sans .txt) matches a key in source-refs.yaml; (4) validates file contents are valid stestr format (no binary, lines are either blank, start with #, or contain a regex pattern). Add the function call to the test runner section at the end of the file. Follow the existing test function pattern with PASS/FAIL counters and assert_* helpers from tests/lib/assertions.sh.

tasks.title: Add test-service-images job structure tests to verify_build_images_workflow.sh (CC-0034 REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-010, REQ-011, REQ-012)
tasks.description: Add test functions to tests/container-images/verify_build_images_workflow.sh validating the test-service-images job structure. New test functions: (1) test_test_service_images_job_defined — job exists under .jobs; (2) test_test_service_images_matrix — matrix.service includes keystone, matrix.release includes 2025.2; (3) test_test_service_images_fail_fast — fail-fast is false; (4) test_test_service_images_depends_on_base — needs contains build-base-images and verify-base-images; (5) test_test_service_images_permissions — packages: read, contents: read, no id-token/attestations/security-events; (6) test_test_service_images_consumes_venv_builder_output — steps reference needs.build-base-images.outputs.venv-builder-image; (7) test_test_service_images_env_vars — run blocks use env: for matrix values. Add all function calls to the test runner section. Follow existing yq_raw/assert_* patterns.

tasks.title: Add test-service-images step-level tests to verify_build_images_workflow.sh (CC-0034 REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-005, REQ-006, REQ-010)
tasks.description: Add test functions to tests/container-images/verify_build_images_workflow.sh validating the test-service-images step-level structure. New test functions: (1) test_test_service_images_source_ref_step — source-ref step exists with yq and source-refs.yaml; (2) test_test_service_images_patch_step — patch step has hashFiles guard; (3) test_test_service_images_constraint_overrides_step — constraint overrides step references apply-constraint-overrides.sh; (4) test_test_service_images_install_step — install step references uv pip install, [test], stestr, testtools, upper-constraints.txt; (5) test_test_service_images_stestr_run_step — stestr run step exists with stestr init and conditional --exclude-list referencing test-excludes; (6) test_test_service_images_artifact_upload — upload-artifact step exists with if: always() guard and name referencing matrix.service. Add all function calls to the test runner section.

tasks.title: Update existing multi-job tests and add verify-service-images dependency wiring test (CC-0034 REQ-007, REQ-010, REQ-012)
tasks.description: Update existing test functions in tests/container-images/verify_build_images_workflow.sh to account for the new fifth job: (1) Rename test_four_jobs_defined to test_five_jobs_defined and add assert for test-service-images; (2) Update test_timeout_minutes_on_all_jobs to check test-service-images timeout; (3) Update test_runs_on_ubuntu_latest to check test-service-images runner; (4) Update test_matrix_jobs_fail_fast_false to check test-service-images fail-fast; (5) Update or add test_verify_service_images_depends_on_test_service_images to check that verify-service-images needs includes both build-service-images and test-service-images; (6) Update all function calls in the test runner section. Also update test_verify_jobs_no_sbom_permissions and test_verify_jobs_no_security_events_permission to check that test-service-images also lacks these permissions.

tasks.title: Update docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md with test-service-images job reference (CC-0034)
tasks.description: Update docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md to document the new test-service-images job. Add a ### test-service-images section after ### build-service-images (following the existing section pattern). Include: property table (runs-on, timeout-minutes, needs, matrix, permissions), step table (checkout, resolve source ref, checkout upstream, apply patches, apply constraint overrides, docker login, run tests, upload artifacts), description of the test-excludes mechanism, and dependency relationship diagram. Update the existing job dependency diagram at the top of the Jobs section to show the new five-job topology. Update the ### verify-service-images section to document the updated needs array. Add CC-0034 to the feature reference in the YAML frontmatter.

review_criteria: All five jobs (build-base-images, verify-base-images, build-service-images, test-service-images, verify-service-images) are defined in build-images.yaml and the dependency DAG is acyclic: base → verify-base → {build-service, test-service} → verify-service
test-service-images uses least-privilege permissions (contents: read, packages: read only) — no id-token, attestations, or security-events
All run: blocks in test-service-images use env: for matrix values (expression injection defense) — no direct ${{ matrix.* }} in run: strings
verify_build_images_workflow.sh passes with all new test-service-images tests plus updated multi-job tests (job count, timeout, runner, fail-fast, permissions)
verify_release_config.sh passes with test-excludes validation when releases/2025.2/test-excludes/keystone.txt exists
actions/upload-artifact step in test-service-images has if: always() guard so artifacts are available even on test failure
stestr --exclude-list flag is conditional on the exclusion file existing (not hardcoded, not failing when absent)
docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md has a complete ### test-service-images section with property table, step table, and updated dependency diagram
All new files have SPDX license headers matching existing repository convention
actions/upload-artifact is pinned to a 40-char SHA with a version comment (matching existing action pinning convention)
implementation_notes:
Architecture: The test-service-images job runs in parallel with build-service-images (both depend on verify-base-images). It uses docker run with the venv-builder image to install test dependencies and execute stestr. This avoids modifying Dockerfiles or adding test tooling to production images.

Dependency DAG:
```
build-base-images
      |
      v
verify-base-images
      |
      +---------------------+
      |                     |
      v                     v
build-service-images   test-service-images
      |                     |
      +---------------------+
      |
      v
verify-service-images (push only)
```

Docker run strategy: The venv-builder image has uv and a virtualenv at /var/lib/openstack. The docker run command mounts: (1) src/<service> for the source tree (writable, stestr creates .stestr/), (2) releases/<release>/upper-constraints.txt for dependency constraints, (3) test-excludes file (optional, read-only), (4) test-results directory (for subunit output). Inside the container: uv pip install --prefix /var/lib/openstack --constraint ... '<service>[test]' stestr testtools, then stestr init, stestr run [--exclude-list ...], stestr last --subunit > results.

Expression injection defense: All matrix values in run: blocks are passed via env: (matching build-service-images pattern, lines 298-301, 321-322, 326-327).

Patterns to follow:
- Source preparation steps: build-images.yaml lines 294-328 (build-service-images)
- Shell test functions: tests/container-images/verify_build_images_workflow.sh (yq_raw + assert_* helpers)
- Release config validation: tests/container-images/verify_release_config.sh (test_* functions with PASS/FAIL counters)
- SPDX headers: line 1-3 pattern in all shell scripts and YAML files

Potential pitfalls:
1. The venv-builder image may run as root — ensure the docker run user is compatible with writing to /var/lib/openstack and the source directory
2. stestr may require python-subunit to be installed for --subunit output — include in the install command if needed
3. The test timeout should be generous (60 minutes) since upstream OpenStack keystone has thousands of unit tests
4. actions/upload-artifact must use a SHA-pinned reference with version comment (matching existing convention)
5. The exclude-list file path inside the docker container must match the mount target
